desc: Tamraght is a surf-focused Berber village just north of Agadir, popular for relaxed cafés, yoga stays, and easy access to Taghazout Bay breaks.
hls: Good base for yoga and remote-work surf camps

todo.name: Imourane surf session
todo.desc: Book a lesson on one of the area's most accessible breaks.

todo.name: Banana Beach sunset walk
todo.desc: Spend late afternoon on the broad beach stretch south of village.

todo.name: Paradise Valley half-day
todo.desc: Visit pools and canyon viewpoints inland from Agadir.

todo.name: Tamraght hillside cafés
todo.desc: Try Moroccan breakfasts and smoothie bowls in surf cafés.

todo.name: Agadir souk evening trip
todo.desc: Use a short transfer to explore Souk El Had.

todo.name: Taghazout skatepark + promenade
todo.desc: Pair skatepark visit with harborfront walk and fish grills.

wifi.note: Many surf camps offer workable Wi-Fi, but speeds vary by occupancy.

prac.fltNote: UK has strong seasonal nonstop coverage to Agadir, while Ireland/other departures may connect via European hubs; winter sun season and Easter weeks are highest demand.
prac.currency: Morocco local currency (check live rates before booking).
prac.lang: Local language plus tourism-level English in main visitor areas.
prac.tz: Check local timezone and DST rules for exact travel dates.
prac.visa: Confirm current entry/transit rules for your passport before booking.
alerts: Surf conditions vary quickly with swell and tide—confirm beginner safety windows daily.
Village roads are narrow with mixed pedestrian/vehicle traffic; walk cautiously at night.
Some accommodations rely on shared shuttle schedules, reducing flexibility for early flights.

sweet: Oct-Apr for consistent surf swells and mild daytime temperatures.
